content.full_text:
City of Pico Rivera

6615 PASSONS BOULEVARD · PICO RIVERA, CALIFORNIA 90660
P.O. Box 1016                (213) 942-2000 • (213) 723-3191

September 7, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attn: John H. Larson, Chairman
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Larson:

This City had a candidate in the General Municipal Election who was one month late in filing his campaign statement ending June 30, 1990 (due to be filed by July 31, 1990). This matter was referred to the office of the District Attorney per Section 91001 of the Political Reform Act and also from the Handbook for City and County Filing Officers of 1989.

The candidate has since came to the office and filed and also paid a fine imposed upon him, however, we notified the District Attorneys office in order that this matter be terminated.

The District Attorneys office called on September 6, 1990 to say that the Political Reform Act Code was voted through an initiative, was very ambiguous and could not be enforced by the District Attorneys Office.

Section 91001 (a) of the Political Reform Act states that "the district attorney of any county in which a violation occurs has concurrent powers and responsibilities with the Attorney General".

The question is, does the District Attorneys Office have the authority to enforce Section 91001 of the Political Reform Act or is it the filing officers responsibility.

If further information is needed, please do not hesitate to contact this office at (213) 942-2000 ext. 285.

Very truly yours,

Thelma M. Kail
Thelma M. Kail, CMC
City Clerk

TMK:ei

cc: City Attorney
